07 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Maritime Interdiction Operation, Jan. 7, 2026 SOUTHCOM 07 Jan 2026 -- In a pre-dawn action this morning, the Department of War, in coordination with the Department of Homeland Security, apprehended a stateless, sanctioned dark fleet motor tanker without incident.
  • 'Outright piracy': Moscow slams US seizure of Russian-flagged oil tanker in North Atlantic Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Russia has condemned the US capture of a Russian-flagged oil tanker in the Atlantic as a violation of the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea.
  • US Seizes 'Stateless' Russian-Flagged Tanker After Lengthy Chase Across Atlantic RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- US naval forces boarded and seized a runaway Russian shadow fleet tanker in the North Atlantic, the latest in a series of moves by Washington against vessels linked to illegal trade in Venezuelan oil.
  • Russia deploys submarine as US attempts to seize oil tanker in Atlantic Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Russia has reportedly dispatched a submarine and several naval vessels to escort an oil tanker being pursued by the US Coast Guard in the North Atlantic on Wednesday.
  • UK provides support to U.S. seizure of Bella 1 accused of shadow fleet activities and Iran sanctions breaches UK MOD 07 Jan 2026 -- The UK has provided enabling support to the United States at their request to interdict the vessel Bella 1 today.

  • Venezuela to deliver 30-50 million barrels of oil to US to be sold by Washington: Trump Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- President Donald Trump has claimed that Venezuela will send 30 to 50 million barrels of oil to the US, with Washington controlling the proceeds, days after it abducted the country's President Nicolas Maduro.
  • US warns Venezuela's security chief next target if uncooperative Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump has reportedly warned Venezuela's Interior Minister Diosdado Cabello that he could become a primary target of US action unless he cooperates with Washington's plans and backs Interim President Delcy Rodríguez in maintaining order.
  • Trump says Venezuela to turn over 30-50 mln barrels of oil to U.S. People's Daily 07 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Venezuelan authorities "will be turning over" 30 million to 50 million barrels of oil to the United States, proceeds from which will be "used to benefit the people of Venezuela and the United States."

  • UN experts condemn US aggression against Venezuela OHCHR 07 Jan 2026 -- United Nations experts* today strongly condemned the large-scale military action carried out by the United States against the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela, the bombardment of Caracas and other cities and the forcible abduction of President Nicolás Maduro and his wife.

  • 'Bullying act': China slams US demand for exclusive Venezuela oil partnership Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- China has denounced as a "typical bullying" act Washington's demand that Venezuela form an "exclusive partnership" in the oil sector with the United States.
  • China's legitimate rights and interests in Venezuela must be safeguarded, Chinese FM responds to claim about US to sell Venezuelan sanctioned oil Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Venezuela is a sovereign state with full and permanent sovereignty over its natural resources and economic activities. The relevant demands of the US violate international law, infringe on Venezuela's sovereignty, and undermine the rights of the Venezuelan people, said Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning on Wednesday, in response to a question regarding that US President Donald Trump claimed that the US will obtain 50 million barrels of previously sanctioned oil from Venezuela, which will be sold by the US on behalf of Venezuela. Some of the crude oil was originally likely to be sold directly to China.
  • Chinese FM on whether China to take actions to safeguard interests in Western Hemisphere after US actions in Venezuela Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- China will continue to offer mutual support on issues concerning each other's core interests and major concerns, such as national sovereignty, security, and territorial integrity, continue to support each other in pursuing development paths suited to their national conditions, jointly oppose power politics, and jointly uphold peace and stability in the region, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Wednesday in response to a question on whether China will take additional actions to protect its interests in the Western Hemisphere considering that moves similar to US actions in Venezuela may occur upon other countries in the Western Hemisphere.
  • Chinese FM responds to question of whether China plans to take any action to help secure release of President Maduro and his wife Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- China has consistently opposed unilateral sanctions lacking a basis in international law or authorization from the UN Security Council, and opposed external forces interfering in Venezuela's internal affairs under any pretext, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning stated Wednesday when addressing a question noting that recent US Department of Justice documents have dropped claims that the "Cartel de los Soles" is an actual group, while Washington has justified its military action against Venezuela by citing the alleged existence of drug cartels, and whether Chinese side plans to take any action to help secure the release of President Maduro and his wife.
  • Chinese FM responds to US claims that 'this is our hemisphere,' saying dividing spheres of influence will not make a country more secure Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- When asked to comment on the claims by US Department of State that "This is OUR Hemisphere, and President Trump will not allow our security to be threatened," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Wednesday at a press conference that dividing spheres of influence and creating geopolitical confrontation will not make a country more secure, nor will it bring peace to the world. Shared security and cooperative security are the only sustainable paths to security, she added.
  • China condemns US demands for Venezuela to partner exclusively on oil production as 'bullying,' breaches of intl law: FM Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Venezuela is a sovereign state and has full permanent sovereignty over all its natural resources and economic activities. The US blatantly used force against Venezuela and asked the country to "favor" America with regard to its oil reserves. Such bullying seriously breaches international law, infringes on Venezuela's sovereignty, and violates the rights of the Venezuelan people. China strongly condemns this, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Wednesday.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • White House: US examining 'a range of options' including military to take Greenland Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- The White House says President Donald Trump and his advisers are looking into "a range of options" in an effort to acquire Greenland and that using the US military to do so is "always an option".
  • Frozen Ambitions: Why America Wants Greenland RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- Amid fallout from the US military ouster of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro on January 3, a war of words has again broken out over the Danish territory of Greenland. US President Donald Trump has repeatedly declared that Washington needs control of the territory and has not ruled out capturing it by force.
  • France, Germany, Poland weigh response to possible US invasion of Greenland Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- France says it is coordinating with Germany and Poland on a joint response after the White House revealed that President Donald Trump is weighing "a range of options" to capture Greenland, including the possible use of military force against the Danish territory.
  • U.S. "almost starting a war with an allied country" in pursuit of Greenland: Danish lawmaker People's Daily 07 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump's administration is "almost starting a war with an allied country" in its pursuit of Greenland, Danish lawmaker Rasmus Jarlov said Tuesday on CNN.
  • China consistently advocates handling intl relations in line with UN Charter: FM on US' claim of taking over Greenland Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- China has consistently advocated handling relations between countries in accordance with the purposes and principles of the Charter of the United Nations, the Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Wednesday in response to a question that the US has once again stated recently that it is considering taking military action to seize Greenland, and has again claimed that such a move would be aimed at safeguarding its national security and curbing China.
  • Trump weighs options to acquire Greenland including use of U.S. military: White House People's Daily 07 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump and his team are weighing "a range of options" to acquire Denmark's Greenland, including "utilizing the U.S. military," White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said in a statement on Tuesday.
  • Denmark's FM rejects US claims about Greenland 'covered' with Chinese ships; Washington cannot cover up hegemonic behavior: expert Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Denmark's Foreign Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen has rejected US President Donald Trump's claims that the US needs Greenland and that it is "covered" with Chinese ships, Reuters reported on Wednesday. A Chinese expert said that Washington cannot cover up its hegemonic behavior by smearing China.

  • PRIORITIZING THE WARFIGHTER IN DEFENSE CONTRACTING The White House 07 Jan 2026 -- "While the United States produces the best military equipment in the world, we do not make enough of it quickly enough to meet the needs of our military and our partners. (...) Effective immediately, they are not permitted in any way, shape, or form to pay dividends or buy back stock, until such time as they are able to produce a superior product, on time and on budget."
  • PRIORITIZING THE WARFIGHTER IN DEFENSE CONTRACTING The White House 07 Jan 2026 -- Fact Sheets
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth Remarks to the Press US Dept. of State 07 Jan 2026
  • Space Flag 26-1 expands, advances electromagnetic warfare tactics United States Space Force 07 Jan 2026 -- Space Training and Readiness Command concluded its largest iteration of Space Flag, Dec. 19, 2025, challenging Guardians as well as joint partners in a contested environment designed to mirror real-world conflict.

  • Declaration of intent between the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, The French Republic and Ukraine relating to the deployment of multinational forces in support of the defence, reconstruction and strategic sustainability of Ukraine Gov.UK 07 Jan 2026
  • Defence Secretary statement on US interception of BELLA 1 and Coalition of the Willing meeting UK MOD 07 Jan 2026 -- The Defence Secretary updated the House of Commons on UK assistance to the US military operation to intercept the Motor Vessel BELLA 1, as well as the recent Coalition of the Willing meeting in Paris.
  • Secretary General joins meeting of the Coalition of the Willing NATO 07 Jan 2026 -- On Tuesday (6 January 2026), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte joined leaders during a meeting of the Coalition of the Willing in Paris. The meeting was co-hosted by the President of France Emmanuel Macron, and the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom Sir Keir Starmer.
  • More weapons for Ukraine: UkrOboronProm increased production by 1.5 times in 2025 Ukraine MOD 07 Jan 2026
  • Ukraine's ground robot fleet expands with the addition of the Zmiy Droid 12.7 armored machine-gun system Ukraine MOD 07 Jan 2026
  • Ukraine transitions to digital planning for humanitarian demining: priorities set for 2026 Ukraine MOD 07 Jan 2026
  • Denys Shmyhal: Military units receive over 1,500 anti-Shahed drones daily Ukraine MOD 07 Jan 2026

  • Chinese aircraft manufacturer hosts first flights of 2026 for J-35 stealth fighter jet, others Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- China's top aircraft manufacturer, the Aviation Industry Corporation of China (AVIC), on Wednesday announced that it had hosted first flights of the new year for the J-35 carrier-based stealth fighter jet among other types of aircraft, accompanied by photos and a video. An expert said the move demonstrated China's confidence in its warplane production capabilities.

  • Air Force to urge U.S. to speed up collision-avoidance system work following F-16V incident CNA 07 Jan 2026 -- Taiwan's Air Force will urge the United States to expedite work to install automatic ground collision avoidance systems (Auto-GCAS) on its F-16V jets, with the aim of completing the process by the end of this year if possible, a military official said Wednesday after an F-16V jet went missing a day earlier.
  • Timeline of key events of Taiwan Air Force F-16 fleet CNA 07 Jan 2026 -- The disappearance from radar of an F-16V fighter jet during a nighttime training mission off eastern Taiwan on Tuesday has renewed attention on Taiwan's F-16 fleet.
  • Air Force cites computer malfunction as search continues for missing F-16V, pilot CNA 07 Jan 2026 -- Taiwan's Air Force on Wednesday linked an incident involving an F-16V fighter jet that went missing a day earlier to a malfunction of the aircraft's main onboard computer.
  • Air Force unable to confirm if F-16 pilot ejected before aircraft disappeared CNA 07 Jan 2026 -- The Air Force said Wednesday that the pilot of a missing F-16V fighter jet had indicated he would eject from the aircraft before it disappeared from radar, but could not confirm whether the ejection was successful.

  • Combined strike capability on show Australia DOD 07 Jan 2026 -- The Australian Army, United States Army and United States Air Force demonstrated a new capability as part of Exercise Kenney Strikes Back.

  • LONG RANGE TRAINING DEPLOYMENT OF INDIAN NAVY'S FIRST TRAINING SQUADRON (1TS) TO SOUTH EAST ASIA India PIB 07 Jan 2026 -- The ships of the First Training Squadron (1TS) of the Indian Navy — INS Tir, Shardul, Sujata and ICGS Sarathi — will be proceeding on a Long Range Training Deployment (LRTD) to South East Asia as part of a training curriculum of the 110th Integrated Officers' Training Course (IOTC).

  • Enemies' threats against Iran will not go unanswered: Army chief IRNA 07 Jan 2026 -- Chief Commander of the Iranian Army Major General Amir Hatami says Iran views the escalating rhetoric of its adversaries as a direct threat and will respond firmly if such threats continue.
  • Iran army chief: Enemy threats will not go unanswered Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Army's chief commander has warned that escalating rhetoric and threats by Iran's enemies will be regarded as a direct threat and will not go unanswered, noting that any mistake by adversaries would trigger a decisive response.
  • 'Security a red line': Iran vows 'decisive' response to intensified threats Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Defense Council has condemned escalating threats, rhetoric and meddlesome remarks - particularly on the part of the United States - against the Islamic Republic, warning that any attack on the country's security, independence, or territorial integrity will trigger a proportionate and decisive response.

  • Minister of National Defense Visits ROK-U.S. Combined Forces Command for First On-Site Inspection of the New Year ROK MND 07 Jan 2026

  • Serbia Deepens Military Ties With China Through Drones, Air Defense Systems RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- Amid growing tensions between global powers and continued instability in the Western Balkans, Serbia has intensified its military cooperation with China, acquiring advanced drones and air defense systems that signal a deepening strategic alignment.

  • The Norwegian Armed Forces Medical Services in Transition Forsvaret - Norwegian Armed Forces 07 Jan 2026 -- As of 1 January 2026, the Norwegian Armed Forces Medical Service (NAFMS) has been given a new organisational structure. In light of the security situation, both the medical profession and operational deliveries will be strengthened.

  • In Germany, the President underscored the importance of German leadership in ensuring the security of Lithuania and the entire eastern flank President of the Republic of Lithuania 07 Jan 2026 -- On Wednesday, President Gitanas Nausėda took part in the annual meeting of the Christian Social Union (CSU) parliamentary group of the Bundestag in Germany, where issues of European security were discussed.
  • Keynote address by Gitanas Nausėda, President of the Republic of Lithuania, at a closed meeting of the CSU parliamentary group of the German Bundestag President of the Republic of Lithuania 07 Jan 2026

  • 2026 GAA now allows borrowing to speed up defense upgrades Philippine News Agency 07 Jan 2026 -- The 2026 General Appropriations Act (GAA) allows the government to borrow funds to modernize defense equipment, a policy shift aimed at accelerating upgrades while easing pressure on the national budget, a senator said Wednesday.

  • Withdrawing the United States from International Organizations, Conventions, and Treaties that Are Contrary to the Interests of the United States The White House 07 Jan 2026 -- "I hereby direct all executive departments and agencies (agencies) to take immediate steps to effectuate the withdrawal of the United States from the organizations listed in section 2 of this memorandum as soon as possible. For United Nations entities, withdrawal means ceasing participation in or funding to those entities to the extent permitted by law."
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Withdraws the United States from International Organizations that Are Contrary to the Interests of the United States The White House 07 Jan 2026
  • Withdrawal from Wasteful, Ineffective, or Harmful International Organizations US Dept. of State 07 Jan 2026 -- Today, in furtherance of Executive Order 14199, President Trump announced the withdrawal of the United States from 66 international organizations identified as part of the Trump Administration's review of wasteful, ineffective, and harmful international organizations.
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with G7 Foreign Ministers US Dept. of State 07 Jan 2026
  • PM call with President Trump of the United States: 7 Jan 2026 Gov.UK 07 Jan 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud US Dept. of State 07 Jan 2026

  • 7 January 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 07 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Construction of the Eastern Border Security Barrier Expands Israel MOD 07 Jan 2026 -- As part of the construction work on the Eastern Border Security Barrier, led by the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) Security Fence and Border Administration, the Israel National Mine Action Authority (INMAA) conducted a controlled detonation of three minefields.

  • PM Netanyahu on a tour of the Western Negev: "We are coming to return the Negev to the State of Israel. This means settlement on a scale we haven't known, but first and foremost - restoring law and order." Israel - Prime Minister 07 Jan 2026
  • Netanyahu Announces Soon Transition to 2nd Phase of Trump's Gaza Peace Plan Sputnik 07 Jan 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said that the first phase of US President Donald Trump's peace plan had already been nearly implemented and that the parties would shortly move on to its second phase, calling for the disarmament of Palestinian movement Hamas and the demilitarization of the enclave.
  • Netanyahu Claims Peace With Palestinians, Arab World Achievable Without Palestinian State Sputnik 07 Jan 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu once again rejected the idea of establishing the State of Palestine alongside Israel, saying that peace between Israel and the Arab world, including the Palestinians, could be achieved without a Palestinian state.

  • Israel approves over 3,400 settler units in occupied East al-Quds Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Israeli authorities have issued a tender to build 3,401 new settler units east of occupied al-Quds, despite international condemnation of Tel Aviv's ongoing settlement expansion.

  • West Bank faces fragmentation under Israel's 2026 settlement map IRNA 07 Jan 2026 -- The Israeli regime is reportedly attempting to redraw the map of its settlements in various parts of the West Bank, which would further fragment the region and increase disconnection among Palestinians.

  • One killed, another injured in Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon amid ceasefire violations Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- At least one person has been killed and another injured when the Israeli military conducted an airstrike against an area in Lebanon's South Governorate, amid the Tel Aviv regime's repeated violations of a ceasefire in place since late 2024.

  • UN report chronicles intensification of decades of severe racial discrimination by Israel in occupied West Bank OHCHR 07 Jan 2026 -- A comprehensive UN Human Rights Office report released today details the asphyxiating impact of Israel's laws, policies and practices on every aspect of daily life for Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem. The report warns that Israel is violating international law requiring States to prohibit and eradicate racial segregation and apartheid.
  • West Bank: UN report warns of 'systematic asphyxiation' of Palestinian rights UN News 07 Jan 2026 -- A sweeping UN human rights report released on Wednesday concludes that in the occupied West Bank, Israel is violating international law prohibiting racial segregation and apartheid, warning that the discriminatory practices have accelerated dramatically since late 2022 amid growing violence, repression and impunity.

  • Iran: Israeli regime's attempt to disintegrate Somalia a dangerous heresy in international relations IRNA 07 Jan 2026 -- The spokesperson for Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs has condemned the Israeli regime's measure to disintegrate Somalia, calling it a dangerous heresy and a serious blow to the foundations of international law and norms.

  • Joint statement on support to UNRWA and NGOs Government of Norway 07 Jan 2026 -- Iceland, Ireland, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, Slovenia and Spain condemn the latest Israeli legislation targeting UNRWA, including measures to cut water, electricity and communications to its facilities.
  • The General Secretariat of the OIC condemns Israeli occupation forces' raid on Birzeit University and assault on students and staff Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 07 Jan 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) condemns in the strongest terms the military raid carried out by Israeli occupation forces on the campus of Birzeit University in the Ramallah governorate, and the accompanying systematic assaults on students, academics, and staff working there, which resulted in numerous injuries among them

    • Other Conflicts

  • PH Navy ready for possible Chinese incursions in 2026 Philippine News Agency 07 Jan 2026 -- A ranking naval official said the Philippine Navy (PN) is ready for the possibility that Chinese maritime forces might carry out more incursions in the country's waters, especially the West Philippine Sea (WPS), this year.
  • Strong trust for allies in WPS shows Filipinos' raised awareness Philippine News Agency 07 Jan 2026 -- A ranking naval official said the Stratbase-commissioned Pulse Asia survey that had Filipinos overwhelmingly trust the country's allies in the ongoing West Philippine Sea (WPS) row with China indicates that the public now understands the issues surrounding the vital waterway.

  • Leader of Yemen's UAE-led separatists flees ahead of talks in Saudi Arabia Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Aidarous al-Zubaidi, head of Yemen's separatist Southern Transitional Council (STC), has reportedly fled to an unknown location as tensions escalate between Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and their former Yemeni ally.
  • Saudi-led coalition strikes southern Yemen, targets arms depot in al-Dhale Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- The Saudi-led coalition supporting Yemen's so-called Presidential Leadership Council (PLC) has carried out airstrikes in the country's southern al-Dhale governorate, targeting a site which was supposedly used as an arms depot by the head of the pro-secession Southern Transitional Council, Aidrous al-Zubaidi.

  • Deadly clashes erupt in Syria's Aleppo as US-backed SDF forces defy HTS rule Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Syria's northern city of Aleppo has witnessed its deadliest clashes yet between Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) regime forces and US-backed fighters, as insecurity persists across the country a year after the ouster of former president Bashar al-Assad.
  • Syria: Aleppo fighting leaves civilians dead, displaces thousands UN News 07 Jan 2026 -- At least five civilians have been killed and some 30,000 people displaced following renewed fighting in Aleppo, Syria, prompting calls from the UN for immediate de-escalation and a return to political talks.

  • Israeli forces raid Quneitra countryside in new violation of Syria's sovereignty Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Israeli military forces have launched a new incursion into several areas in the countryside of Syria's southwestern province of Quneitra, in the latest violation of the country's sovereignty, local media say.

  • The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation strongly condemns the visit of the Foreign Minister of Israel—the occupying power—to the so-called 'Somaliland' region Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 07 Jan 2026 -- The Secretary-General of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), Mr
  • Iran: Israeli FM's visit to Somaliland a 'dangerous precedent' Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry has condemned the visit of Israeli foreign minister Gideon Saar to the self-declared republic of Somaliland, calling it a "dangerous precedent in international relations" and a "lethal blow to the legal and normative foundations of the United Nations."

News Reports

  • The G7 Foreign Minister's Telephone Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 07 Jan 2026

  • Cuba condemns 'hostile' US rhetoric, pledges to defend sovereignty Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Cuba's minister of foreign affairs has strongly denounced the Trump administration's hostile rhetoric toward his country, warning that Havana remains ready to protect its sovereignty in the face of escalating US aggression.

  • Finnish Customs has concluded the preliminary inquiry into a suspected sanctions violation by the crew of the vessel Fitburg Finnish Customs 07 Jan 2026 -- Customs has completed the preliminary inquiry into a possible violation of sanctions by the Fitburg, suspected of involvement in a cable damage incident.
  • The Fitburg Vessel Seized by the National Bureau of Investigation Police of Finland 07 Jan 2026 -- The National Bureau of Investigation has seized the Fitburg vessel, which is suspected of damaging a subsea telecommunications cable in the Baltic Sea.

  • Trump's immigration crackdown turns deadly in Minneapolis; president says killer acted in 'self-defense' Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- A reported immigration enforcement operation carried out under US President Donald Trump's expanded crackdown has ended in a fatal shooting in Minneapolis after an agent opens fire on a woman.

  • US Seizure Of Russian Tanker Puts Shadow Fleet On Notice RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- The US seizure of a Russian-flagged oil tanker in the North Atlantic dramatically raises the stakes in Washington's naval blockade of Venezuela, blowing apart an apparent Kremlin strategy to reflag its shadow fleet ships to offer them protection and stoking wider tensions with Moscow.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview to Yevangelina Berdnikova as part of the New Year Tree of Wishes event, Moscow, January 7, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 Jan 2026
  • Russia's State-Owned Railway Giant Is In Serious Trouble. Blame The Ukraine War. RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- It oversees one of the world's biggest railroad networks, employing around 700,000 people and operating 85,000 kilometers of track that stitches together 11 time zones, carrying millions of passengers and millions of tons of cargo every year.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on January 7, 2026 PRC MofA 07 Jan 2026
  • China will continue to strengthen co-op with Latin American and Caribbean countries, FM says regarding how China will provide further support Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- In response to a question regarding how China will take further action or provide further support, as the response from the Chinese side so far seems to mainly refer to diplomatic support, but for countries like Cuba and Venezuela, they might be expecting to obtain financial or economic support and commitments from China to help sustain their current economies, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Wednesday, "We will continue to strengthen cooperation with the countries you mentioned and other Latin American and Caribbean countries, and we will continue to support each other. As for what specific cooperation or support will be provided, I believe that needs to be jointly determined by China and the relevant countries."
  • China expected to raise Iran oil imports amid Venezuela tensions: Report Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- China's private refiners are expected to increase their imports of Iranian oil amid tensions in Venezuela that have put crude shipments from the country at risk, a report shows.
  • Cooperation should be the defining feature of China-EU ties, says Chinese FM Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Cooperation should be the defining feature of China-EU relations, with partnership being their proper positioning, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Tuesday.
  • China Space Station conducts in-orbit experiment on lithium-ion batteries, aiming to enhance performance of spacecraft energy system: report Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- The Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) revealed that the project titled "In-situ electrochemical optical research on lithium-ion batteries for space applications" has been carried out on the China Space Station, with an ultimate goal to enhance the performance of spacecraft energy system.

  • Maduro-style strike by China unlikely to succeed in Taiwan: Expert CNA 07 Jan 2026 -- A former Pentagon official said Tuesday that China is unlikely to succeed in capturing Taiwan's leaders in a swift aerial military operation due to Taiwan's extensive air defenses system.
  • MAC slams China's labeling of ministers as 'independence advocates' CNA 07 Jan 2026 -- China's move to designate two Taiwan government ministers as "diehard Taiwan independence advocates" undermines cross-strait peace and the status quo, the Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) said Wednesday.
  • Taiwan compatriots recognize 'independence' pursuit only turns themselves into cannon fodder: mainland spokesperson on poll showing 60% Taiwan people unwilling to go to battlefield Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Facts have proven that an increasing number of Taiwan compatriots have come to realize that "Taiwan independence" means war, that the Lai Ching-te authorities' stubborn pursuit of "independence" will only bring conflict and chaos to Taiwan, and that condoning or supporting "Taiwan independence" will only turn themselves and their families into cannon fodder, said Chen Binhua, a spokesperson of the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office on Wednesday.
  • 'Resolving Taiwan question is a matter for Chinese': spokesperson rejects 'concerns' from island over mainland's follow of US approach in Venezuela Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- In response to media inquiry about the US' forcible seizure of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro and his wife and their transfer out of the country, which has drawn widespread attention and prompted some people on Taiwan island to express concern that the Chinese mainland might follow the US approach to "justify taking action against Taiwan," Chen Binhua, the spokesperson from the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said that the US has brazenly used force against a sovereign country, a move that seriously violates international law, infringes on the sovereignty of other states, and threatens regional peace and security. We firmly oppose this.
  • Chinese mainland pledges to advance cause of national reunification People's Daily 07 Jan 2026 -- The Chinese mainland will resolutely oppose "Taiwan independence" separatist activities and external interference, unite Taiwan compatriots, promote the peaceful development of relations across the Taiwan Strait, and advance the cause of national reunification in 2026, a spokesperson said Wednesday.
  • Chinese mainland names two die-hard "Taiwan independence" secessionists People's Daily 07 Jan 2026 -- The Chinese mainland on Wednesday added two individuals to its list of die-hard "Taiwan independence" secessionists, bringing the total number on this list to 14, a spokesperson said.
  • 14 have been listed as diehard 'Taiwan independence' separatists, 12 as accomplices: Taiwan Affairs Office Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- In response to a question that how many people are currently on these lists of diehard Taiwan independence separatists and "Taiwan independence" henchmen and accomplices, and whether the mainland further step up punitive measures and bring them to justice while some public opinion on the island claiming that the mainland's punitive measures have had no substantive impact on these individuals and have instead become so-called "medals" for them to flaunt, a spokesperson from the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council said "Taiwan independence" elements are illegal criminals who seek to split the country and undermine cross-Straits relations.
  • Two more listed as diehard 'Taiwan independence' separatists, one more listed as accomplice and henchman: spokesperson Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Two individuals in the island of Taiwan-Liu Shyh-fang and Cheng Ying-yao-have been listed as diehard "Taiwan independence" separatists and will be subject to punishment in accordance with the law, Chen Binhua, spokesperson from the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, announced on Wednesday.
  • Chinese FM expounds China's position on the Taiwan question, points out regressive moves taken by Japan's current leader Global Times 07 Jan 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi expounded China's position on the Taiwan question and pointed out the regressive moves taken by Japan's current leader, expressing the hope that Finland would understand China's legitimate stance, according to the Xinhua News Agency.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 07 Jan 2026

  • President touts 'irreversibly solidified' ties to top Chinese officials KOREA.net 07 Jan 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on Jan. 6 during his state visit to China attended talks with two high-ranking Chinese leaders on specific methods for the "mature" development of the strategic cooperative partnership between both countries.

  • Activists In Iran Call For Internal Change, Not Foreign Intervention, As Protests Rage On RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- What initially started as protests over economic grievances has snowballed into the biggest threat to Iran's clerical rulers in several years.
  • Interview: Could There Be A 'Venezuela-Like Situation' In Iran? RFE/RL 07 Jan 2026 -- Iran has been rocked by nearly two weeks of nationwide protests, in the most serious challenge to the clerical establishment in several years.
  • Iran security forces foil foreign-linked sedition network, arrest armed suspects Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Iran's security forces have announced a series of arrests in Tehran and two provinces following separate security incidents involving organized unrest and armed activity.
  • Key Mossad operative executed over transfer of sensitive Iranian information Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- A key Mossad operative in Iran, who had transferred sensitive information to the Israelis, was executed after being convicted of espionage on behalf of the terrorist Mossad service, according to the Judiciary.

  • Iraq 'unrecognisable and remarkable' after years of conflict: UN coordinator UN News 07 Jan 2026 -- Some two decades after a shaky political transition, Iraq today is a country "at peace, with increased security and a clear determination to win the battle of development," says the UN resident coordinator for Iraq on Wednesday.

  • Syria's HTS-led regime, Israel to set up joint intelligence-sharing cell Press TV 07 Jan 2026 -- Syria's HTS-led regime and Israel will set up a joint group under US supervision to share intelligence and seek military de-escalation on the ground.

  • PM receives a telephone call from Prime Minister of Israel India MEA 07 Jan 2026

  • Meeting of Weimar Triangle and India foreign ministers Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 Jan 2026 -- An extended-format gathering of the Weimar Triangle's top diplomats focused on shared efforts towards ending Russia's war against Ukraine and on cooperation between the European Union and India.

  • Secretary Lazaro Visits Myanmar, Exchanges Views with Senior General Min Aung Hlaing and other Officials of the State Security and Peace Commission Philippines DFA 07 Jan 2026 -- Secretary of Foreign Affairs Ma. Theresa P. Lazaro, who was appointed by President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. as Special Envoy of the ASEAN Chair on Myanmar, visited Myanmar to meet with the senior leadership of the State Security and Peace Commission in Nay Pyi Taw on 06 January 2026.
